51792d75b7f2.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
51792d75b7f2.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
51792d75b7f2.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

681a05056c09.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
681a05056c09.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

c4a1a24eb5a3.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
c4a1a24eb5a3.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
c4a1a24eb5a3.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

fb5b601f31eb.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
fb5b601f31eb.description: Your 4.8/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P28.6). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
fb5b601f31eb.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

14a4a4f194c9.title: Ranked #6 of 8 locally — peers are ahead
14a4a4f194c9.description: At percentile 28.6, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

631c90b313c0.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
631c90b313c0.description: At 116 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
